pickup_verification_recap.result_v0b: 3b9b1b8 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v2 ... (PASS)
pickup_verification_recap.result_v0c: 8 entries: M pyproject.toml, M tools/autonomy_budget/config.yaml, M tools/autonomy_budget/config_loader.py, M tools/autonomy_budget/tests/test_config_loader.py, ?? tools/_project_context.py, ?? tools/_vikunja_client.py, ?? tools/autonomy_budget/projects/, ?? tools/tests/ (PASS)
pickup_verification_recap.result_v0f: fleet_paused=true, reason "platform separation v2 in progress" (PASS)
pickup_verification_recap.result_v0g: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- NOT POPPED (PASS)

required_acknowledgments_verbatim.ack: I will incorporate all 8 corrections into a re-emitted comprehension gate before any item 2.4 work begins.
I will use `-BlarAIRoot` (not `-ProjectRoot`) for all 7 PS1 parameters per v2 canonical signature pattern at 2.4.v2.

I will explicitly enumerate the 12 Python entrypoints (9 load_config callers from v2-1 + 3 v2.4.v2 additions) and the 7 PS1 scripts (with la_merge_approve at the correct `tools/scheduled-tasks/` path).
I will include `tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py` MCP handlers in item 2.4 audit and extend item 2.6 grep patterns per C4/C6 source citations.
If item 2.8 `$BLARAI_PID` preflight returns empty or any value other than 3, I will STOP and report to Lead Architect — no silent regex or yaml adaptation.
I will use descriptive risk labels (no numeric prefix overlap with AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW or verification-block IDs).
I will not begin item 2.4 work, create branches, or run mutation commands until the re-emitted gate is reviewed and explicitly authorized by Guide instance 4.

critical_carryovers.ack: Stage 2 produces exactly ONE commit (item 2.9). All intermediate work stays uncommitted.
Fleet stays paused throughout Stage 2. NEVER call state.resume_fleet(). Fleet remains paused through Stage 4 cutover.
No merge to main during Stage 2. Branch chore/platform-extraction is the only working surface.
stash@{0} (wake_launcher auto-stash on main) is NOT to be popped or applied.
No edits to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or any A1-A10 governance doc during Stage 2.
All Vikunja access flows through tools/_vikunja_client.py. list_tasks / search_tasks MUST receive int project_id. bool is rejected before int (cleanup-C3).
_project_context.resolve() is the single source for ProjectContext in every refactored entrypoint. Hardcoded C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI paths in entrypoints are a BUG.
V8 defaults preservation: pre-existing pytest discovery, fleet behavior on the BlarAI path, and CLI default behavior all unchanged when --project-root is omitted.
STATUS.md updates at item 2.10 are APPEND-ONLY. Do not edit prior Stage entries.

plus_guide_3.ack: V1-V7 + V8 verification block before closing handoff.

plus_iteration_1_acks.ack: V0a-V0g pickup verification block must be re-executed (and outputs reported) at the start of every successor session before substantive work begins.
All 10 acknowledgments above (9 from predecessor + 3 from Guide instance 3, deduped) are binding through Stage 4 cutover.
V1-V7 closure verification (per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml) plus per-item V8 (defaults-preservation) must be executed and outputs included in the final closing handoff at Stage 2 completion.

If a successor approaches context exhaustion before item 2.9, it MUST emit a v3 (or higher) mid-stage handoff XML following the same schema as v1/v2, commit ONLY that file (docs-only commit), and stop. NO partial-commit refactor work. NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items.

comprehension_summary.part_a_objective: Complete the BlarAI-side platform-aware refactor of `tools/` so every entrypoint (Python + PowerShell + MCP server) accepts an optional project root + project-id, defaults preserve current BlarAI behavior, all Vikunja access (CLI, scheduled tasks, AND MCP handlers per C4) routes through `tools/_vikunja_client.py` with mandatory `int project_id`, and the 280-test baseline holds. Stage 2 closes with exactly one commit at item 2.9 (folding the 8 already-on-disk paths from items 2.1+2.2+2.3 plus all 2.4-2.8 work) and an append-only STATUS.md update at item 2.10. Branch chore/platform-extraction; no merge to main; fleet stays paused; stash@{0} untouched.

item.title: Python entrypoints: argparse --project-root + --project-id (type=int) + _project_context.resolve()

ent.notes: legacy; ALSO touched by item 2.6 -- consistent treatment per Guide-#4

plus_mcp_server.requirement: Every Vikunja-touching MCP tool handler (FastMCP / JSON-RPC) MUST route through tools/_vikunja_client.py. MCP handlers do not take CLI args; instead the server resolves ProjectContext via _project_context.resolve() at startup (or per-handler from env/config) so an LLM session cannot bypass scope.

item.signature_pattern_python: parser.add_argument('--project-root', type=str, default=None) parser.add_argument('--project-id', type=int, default=None) ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id)

item.title: Verify --help on all 12 Python entrypoints + MCP handler routing
item.action: For each of the 12 entrypoints: run `python -m <module> --help` and confirm both flags appear. For tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py: spawn the server and confirm list_tasks / search_tasks handlers route through tools/_vikunja_client.py (no direct HTTP).

item.title: PowerShell scripts: add -BlarAIRoot parameter (audit scope = 3 directories)
item.audit_scope_side_effect: tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 also exists in the expanded audit scope. NOT on canonical 7. Will inspect at item 2.5 and surface refactor-or-defer decision in closing handoff. Awaiting Guide direction if it should be added.

item.title: Verify each of the 7 PS1 accepts -BlarAIRoot and defaults preserve BlarAI behavior
Vikunja chokepoint audit + replacement (extended grep patterns per Guide-#5/C6)
item.triage_rule: Triage every hit; do not pre-filter. Replace each direct HTTP call with VikunjaClient. Any list/search call site that cannot supply int project_id is a BUG -- fix, not defer.
item.legacy_consistency_rule: tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py is touched by BOTH item 2.4 (load_config caller refactor -> argparse + ProjectContext) AND item 2.6 (chokepoint decision: route through new tools/_vikunja_client.py OR fold in entirely). Treat consistently across both items; surface decision in closing handoff. legacy-name-collision-watch active.

item.title: Defaults-preservation smoke + $BLARAI_PID preflight (w3)
item.on_drift: STOP and report to Lead Architect. NO silent regex/yaml adaptation. (Guide-#8)

item.title: Single Stage 2 commit
item.command: git add -A (or git add tools/ pyproject.toml docs/)
item.hard_requirement: pyproject.toml MUST be included in the commit.

item.title: Append-only STATUS.md Stage 2 COMPLETE marker
item.fields: commit hash, files-touched count, V1-V7+V8 verification summary
item.hard_requirement: Append-only. Do NOT edit prior Stage entries.

part_d_verification_strategy.per_python_entrypoint_v8: Run with no flags after refactor; confirm behavior matches pre-refactor baseline. Repeat for all 12 entrypoints.
part_d_verification_strategy.per_ps1_v8: Invoke with no -BlarAIRoot after refactor; confirm BlarAI default path resolves and script behavior matches pre-refactor baseline. Repeat for all 7 PS1.
part_d_verification_strategy.mcp_handler_check: At 2.4.v2: spawn tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py; verify list_tasks and search_tasks handlers route through tools/_vikunja_client.py (no direct HTTP, no scope bypass).
part_d_verification_strategy.cross_item_v1_v7: At 2.7 / 2.7.v2: execute V1-V7 assertions per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml.
part_d_verification_strategy.commit_gate: Post-2.9: re-run pytest tools/; git status clean; commit hash captured for 2.10.

part_e_risks.risk: Existing scripts may already define --project-root / --project-id; detect and reconcile rather than blindly add.
Regression in any of 9 load_config callers; mitigated by keyword-only project_overlay_path but verify per-entrypoint.
All 7 PS1 scripts must accept -BlarAIRoot; item 2.5 must verify post-refactor.
tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py vs tools/_vikunja_client.py -- import-confusion + dual-touch (2.4 + 2.6) consistency hazard.
If tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py handlers don't route through tools/_vikunja_client.py, an LLM session can issue scope-leaking queries.
Item 2.6 must use the extended pattern set; narrow grep misses real call sites.
If 2.4 surfaces need for additional project-scoped config keys, extend _apply_project_overlay per-key (additive); never introduce generic deep-merge.
yaml type-stub lint warning is benign; do NOT add stubs as runtime dep.
Item 2.8 $BLARAI_PID must resolve to exactly 3; empty or other value -> STOP and report.
Fleet remains paused throughout (V0f confirmed); stash@{0} untouched (V0g confirmed); never call state.resume_fleet() during Stage 2.
If context exhausts before 2.9 -> emit v3 mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit), no partial-commit refactor.

comprehension_summary.part_f_rollback_acknowledgment: If post-item-2.9 verification fails (V1-V7 closure or per-item V8), rollback is `git reset --hard HEAD~1` on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed). Branch is preserved for audit per copilot-instructions preservation_rule. Fleet stays disabled -- no runtime impact from rollback.

on_disk_evidence_for_guide_corrections.evidence: Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter "*.ps1" results: - tools/scheduled-tasks/la_merge_approve.ps1 (CONFIRMED -- not in tools/fleet_ops/) - tools/fleet_observability/escalation_notify.ps1 (CONFIRMED present) - tools/scheduled-tasks/test_async_post_gate.ps1 (CONFIRMED present) - tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 (SURFACED -- audit-scope side effect, not on canonical 7)

ea_comprehension_gate.stop_discipline: STOPPING. Iteration-2 comprehension gate emitted with all 8 Guide-instance-4 corrections incorporated. Awaiting Guide instance 4 re-review. No item 2.4 work, branch creation, or mutation commands will be issued until explicit PROCEED authorization.
ea_comprehension_gate.open_question_for_guide: tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 surfaced via expanded PS1 audit scope. Not enumerated in 2.4.v2 additional_tools or G-1/G-4. Should it be added to canonical 7 (becoming 8), or surfaced as defer-decision in closing handoff? Awaiting direction.
